headline: 85% confident at least 14 items finish in the next 15 days (by May 31, 2026).
definition: Monte Carlo forecast of how many items finish by a target date. The histogram is the distribution of simulated item counts; percentile lines mark confidence thresholds. Read BACKWARD: higher confidence = FEWER items, a more conservative commitment.

summary.reading: backward — higher confidence means FEWER items

horizon.reading: Forecast horizon is within the training window — relatively trusted. Shorter is better.

key_insight: Median outcome is 20 items; 95% commitment floor is 10. Higher confidence = fewer items — promising 10 leaves throughput on the table to buy certainty.
next_actions: For external commitments, promise 14 items (or 10 if stakes are high).
For internal planning, treat 20 as the realistic target — half the time you deliver less.
Re-run weekly; the floor moves as historical throughput shifts.
caveats: Confidence reads BACKWARD: higher % means FEWER items, not more.
Throughput unit is 'completed items' — assumes item size is stable across windows.
No accounting for known disruptions (holidays, on-call rotations, launches).

vocabulary.Throughput: Items completed per unit time (here: per day). The empirical input Monte Carlo Simulation draws from.
vocabulary.Training window: The recent period whose daily throughput we sample. Defaults to the last 30 calendar days ending yesterday-UTC — the recommended horizon.
vocabulary.Monte Carlo Simulation: Draws daily throughput with replacement, simulates forward, and repeats (10,000 runs by default). The distribution of outcomes is the forecast.
vocabulary.Results histogram: The empirical distribution produced by Monte Carlo. X-axis = outcome (date for when-done; item count for how-many); Y-axis = simulation-run frequency.
vocabulary.Percentile: Confidence level. For when-done (date axis): read FORWARD — 85% confidence is a later date. For how-many (items axis): read BACKWARD — 85% confidence is FEWER items, a more conservative commitment.

cli_invocation: uv run flow forecast how-many --repo pytest-dev/pytest --target-date 2026-05-31 --start-date 2026-05-17 --history-start 2026-04-17 --history-end 2026-05-16 --runs 10000 --seed 42
